title: pinguicula gypsicola
intro: Pinguicula gypsicola, also known as the gypsum butterwort, is a species of carnivorous plant belonging to the genus Pinguicula. It is native to Mexico and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pinguicula gypsicola include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be dried and used in herbal infusions for its medicinal properties.
